overview: This appears to be the Blue Pouch Fungus (Clavogaster virescens), characterized by its white, pouch-like or blob-like shape. It is endemic to New Zealand and often found in forest habitats. The surface is often smooth, white when young, and may develop bluish or yellowish tones as it matures. While this specimen is white, some similar-looking fungi in the area can have different features, so careful identification is recommended. iNaturalist +1 2 sites Blue Pouch Fungus (Clavogaster virescens) · iNaturalist

This appears to be the Blue Pouch Fungus (Clavogaster virescens), characterized by its white, pouch-like or blob-like shape.
It is endemic to New Zealand and often found in forest habitats.
The surface is often smooth, white when young, and may develop bluish or yellowish tones as it matures.
While this specimen is white, some similar-looking fungi in the area can have different features, so careful identification is recommended.
